title: Microsoft Intune Manual Device Management
spl:
`azure_monitor_aad` operationName="wipe managedDevice" OR operationName="retire managedDevice" OR operationName="shutdown managedDevice" OR operationName="rebootNow managedDevice"
| rename properties.* AS *
| stats count min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime values(operationName) AS operationName by user, result, targetResources{}.displayName
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
description: Detects manual device management actions in Microsoft Intune such as wipe, retire, or restart, which could indicate an attacker or insider abusing device management capabilities to disrupt endpoints.
value: Detects manual device management actions in Microsoft Intune such as wipe, retire, or restart, which could indicate an attacker or insider abusing device management capabilities to disrupt endpoints.
implementation: Enable Azure Monitor Activity logging for Microsoft Intune operations and forward to Splunk via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services.
detailedImplementation:
Enterprise Security Detection Rule

"Microsoft Intune Manual Device Management" is a Hunting detection designed for analyst-driven threat hunting rather than automated alerting, sourced from the Splunk Enterprise Security Content Update (ESCU). Hunting detections are not intended for automated alerting. They are run on-demand or on a low-frequency schedule by security analysts investigating specific threat hypotheses.

Prerequisites

• Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x or later with the ES Content Update (ESCU) app installed and up to date.
• Data sources: Azure Monitor Activity. Must be ingested into Splunk and normalized to the Common Information Model (CIM) via the appropriate Technology Add-on.
• MITRE ATT&CK: T1021.007, T1072, T1529. Review the ATT&CK matrix for adjacent techniques to identify detection coverage gaps in your environment.

Deployment

1. In Enterprise Security, navigate to Configure → Content → Content Management.
2. Search for "Microsoft Intune Manual Device Management" or filter by Analytic Story.
3. Hunting detections are typically left disabled for automated scheduling. Instead, run them on-demand from the Search bar or schedule at low frequency (daily or weekly).
4. Review results manually — hunting detections cast a wider net and expect analyst judgment to separate signal from noise.
5. When results warrant further investigation, create a Notable Event manually or initiate your incident response workflow.

Tuning and False Positive Management

Known false positives for this detection: Legitimate adminstrative usage of this functionality will trigger this detection.

• Hunting detections are expected to produce broader result sets that require analyst interpretation. Focus on refining the search scope (time range, specific hosts or users) rather than eliminating all noise.
• Maintain a hunting journal documenting hypotheses tested, results found, and detection improvements identified.
• Share high-fidelity findings with the detection engineering team to convert recurring hunting patterns into automated TTP or Anomaly detections.

Analyst Response Workflow

When reviewing Hunting results:

1. Run the search manually or review scheduled results. Evaluate each returned event against your threat hypothesis — not every result indicates compromise.
2. Cross-reference findings with current threat intelligence, recent security advisories, and outputs from other detection sources.
3. If suspicious activity is confirmed, create a Notable Event or escalate directly to your incident response process with the supporting evidence.
4. Document the hunting exercise: hypothesis, data sources queried, time range, findings, and any detection engineering improvements identified.

Validation

Confirm the required data sources are flowing:

```spl
| tstats count where index=* by index, sourcetype | sort -count
```

Verify the detection is enabled and firing:

```spl
index=_audit action="alert_fired" ss_name="*"
| stats count by ss_name, trigger_time | sort -trigger_time
```
knownFalsePositives: Legitimate adminstrative usage of this functionality will trigger this detection.
